content:
# Acts Chapter 9 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF pages 235-237 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ACT_009_page_001.jpg, ACT_009_page_002.jpg, ACT_009_page_003.jpg

---

**1.** But Saul, yet breathing threat and murder against the disciples of the Lord, having approached the high-priest,

**2.** asked from him letters to Damascus to the synagogues, so-that if any he should find being of the Way, both men and women, having-bound [them] he might bring [them] to Jerusalem.

**3.** And in the journeying, it came-to-pass [that] he drew-near to Damascus, and suddenly a light from the heaven flashed-around him,

**4.** and having-fallen upon the ground he heard a voice saying to him, "Saul, Saul, why do you persecute me?"

**5.** And he said, "Who are you, Lord?" And he [said], "I am Jesus whom you persecute.

**6.** But rise-up and enter into the city, and it-will-be-told to you what it-is-necessary [for] you to do."

**7.** And the men, the [ones] traveling-with him, stood speechless, hearing indeed the voice but seeing no-one.

**8.** And Saul was-raised from the ground, and having-been-opened his eyes, he was-seeing nothing; and leading-by-the-hand him they brought [him] into Damascus.

**9.** And he was three days not seeing, and did not eat nor drink.

**10.** And there was a certain disciple in Damascus, by-name Ananias, and the Lord said to him in a vision, "Ananias." And he said, "Behold, I [am here], Lord."

**11.** And the Lord [said] to him, "Having-risen, go upon the street, the [one] being-called Straight, and seek in [the] house of Judas [a man named] Saul, by-name [a man] of Tarsus; for behold, he is-praying,

**12.** and he-saw in a vision a man, by-name Ananias, having-entered and having-laid-on him [his] hands, so-that he might receive-sight."

**13.** And Ananias answered, "Lord, I-have-heard from many concerning this man, how-many evil-things he did to your holy-ones in Jerusalem;

**14.** and here he has authority from the chief-priests to bind all the [ones] calling-upon your name."

**15.** And the Lord said to him, "Go, because a vessel of-election is this [one] to me, to bear my name before nations and kings and sons of Israel;

**16.** for I will-show to him how-many-things it-is-necessary [for] him on-behalf-of my name to suffer."

**17.** And Ananias departed and entered into the house, and having-laid-on him the hands, he said, "Saul, brother, the Lord has-sent me, Jesus, the [one] who-appeared to you on the road by-which you were-coming, so-that you might receive-sight and be-filled [with the] Holy Spirit."

**18.** And immediately there-fell from his eyes as scales, and he received-sight, and having-risen he was-baptized,

**19.** and having-taken food he was-strengthened. And he was with the disciples, the [ones] in Damascus, [for some] days,

**20.** and immediately in the synagogues he was-proclaiming Jesus, that this-one is the Son of God.

**21.** And all the [ones] hearing were-being-amazed and were-saying, "Is not this the [one] having-destroyed in Jerusalem the [ones] calling-upon this name, and here for this [purpose] he-had-come, in-order-that having-bound them he might bring [them] before the chief-priests?"

**22.** And Saul all-the-more was-being-empowered and was-confounding the Jews, the [ones] dwelling in Damascus, proving that this-one is the Christ.

**23.** And as sufficient days were-being-fulfilled, the Jews took-counsel-together to kill him;

**24.** but their plot became-known to Saul. And they-were-also-watching the gates both day and night, so-that they might kill him;

**25.** but the disciples, having-taken him by-night, let-down [him] through the wall, having-lowered [him] in a basket.

**26.** And having-arrived in Jerusalem, he was-attempting to join-himself to the disciples, and all were-fearing him, not believing that he is a disciple.

**27.** But Barnabas, having-taken-hold-of him, brought [him] to the apostles, and related to them how on the road he saw the Lord, and that he spoke to him, and how in Damascus he spoke-boldly in the name of Jesus.

**28.** And he was with them going-in and going-out in Jerusalem, speaking-boldly in the name of the Lord.

**29.** And he was-speaking and was-disputing with the Hellenists; and they were-attempting to kill him.

**30.** And having-known [this], the brothers brought-down him to Caesarea and sent-out him to Tarsus.

**31.** The assembly then throughout the-whole-of Judea and Galilee and Samaria was-having peace, being-built-up, and going in the fear of the Lord and in the encouragement of the Holy Spirit, was-being-multiplied.

**32.** And it came-to-pass [that] Peter, passing-through through all [regions], came-down also to the holy-ones, the [ones] dwelling [in] Lydda.

**33.** And he found there a certain man, by-name Aeneas, from eight years lying upon a mat, who was having-been-paralyzed.

**34.** And Peter said to him, "Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you; rise-up and spread for yourself." And immediately he rose-up.

**35.** And all the [ones] dwelling [in] Lydda and the Sharon saw him, who turned to the Lord.

**36.** And in Joppa there was a certain disciple, by-name Tabitha, which being-translated is-called Dorcas; this-woman was full of good works and acts-of-mercy which she was-doing.

**37.** And it came-to-pass in those days [that] having-become-sick, she died; and having-washed [her], they placed [her] in an upper-room.

**38.** And Lydda being near to Joppa, the disciples, having-heard that Peter is in it, sent two men to him, urging, "Do not delay to come-through to us."

**39.** And Peter, having-risen, went-with them; whom having-arrived they brought-up into the upper-room, and all the widows stood-beside him, weeping and showing tunics and garments, as-many-as Dorcas was-making while being with them.

**40.** And having-put-out all [of them] outside, Peter, having-placed the knees, prayed; and having-turned to the body, he said, "Tabitha, rise-up." And she opened her eyes, and having-seen Peter, she sat-up.

**41.** And having-given her [his] hand, he raised her up; and having-called the holy-ones and the widows, he presented her living.

**42.** And it became known throughout the-whole-of Joppa, and many believed upon the Lord.

**43.** And it came-to-pass [that] he remained sufficient days in Joppa with a certain Simon, a tanner.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

### Manuscript Observations (Codex Sinaiticus)

1. **Verse 5-6:** Sinaiticus omits the longer reading found in later manuscripts: "It is hard for you to kick against the goads" and Saul's response "Lord, what do you wish me to do?" These additions appear in the Textus Receptus tradition (likely harmonized from Acts 26:14 and 22:10) but are absent from Sinaiticus and other early Alexandrian witnesses (Vaticanus, P74). The Sinaiticus reading moves directly from "I am Jesus whom you persecute" to "But rise up and enter into the city."

2. **Verse 7:** Sinaiticus reads the accusative for "voice" (THNFWNHN), whereas in Acts 22:9 the genitive is used. This creates the well-known tension between the two accounts -- in 9:7 the companions "hear the voice" (accusative), while in 22:9 they "did not hear the voice" (genitive). I have preserved the case distinction faithfully.

3. **Verse 8:** Sinaiticus reads "having-been-opened his eyes" (ANEWGMENWN TWN OFQALMWN), an unusual genitive absolute construction suggesting his eyes were open yet he saw nothing -- not that his eyes were closed.

4. **Verse 12:** Sinaiticus includes "in a vision" (EN ORAMATI) describing Saul's seeing of Ananias. Some later manuscripts omit this phrase.

5. **Verse 17:** The phrase order "the Lord has sent me, Jesus" is preserved as found in the manuscript. The syntax is ambiguous -- "Jesus" stands in apposition to "the Lord" but its placement after "sent me" creates a deliberate grammatical roughness I have not smoothed.

6. **Verse 20:** Sinaiticus reads "the Son of God" (TON UION TOU QEOU), which is the reading also of P74 and Vaticanus. Some Western witnesses read "the Christ" here instead.

7. **Verse 25:** Sinaiticus reads "the disciples" (OI MAQHTAI) as the subject -- i.e., his disciples took him. The reading "his disciples" (OI MAQHTAI AUTOU) appears in some witnesses; Sinaiticus lacks the pronoun AUTOU here.

8. **Verse 31:** Sinaiticus reads the singular "the assembly" (H EKKLHSIA) and singular verbs, treating the church as one unified entity across Judea, Galilee, and Samaria. Some later manuscripts read the plural "the assemblies" (AI EKKLHSIAI). The singular is the harder reading and almost certainly original.

9. **Verse 33:** The duration "from eight years" (EX ETWN OKTWN) is rendered literally. English would naturally say "for eight years," but the Greek preposition EX indicates the starting point of his condition.

10. **Verse 34:** Sinaiticus reads "spread for yourself" (STRWSON SEAUTW), referring to making his own bed/mat. Some translations interpret this as "make your bed," but the literal sense is "spread [it] for yourself."

11. **Verse 38:** Sinaiticus reads "two men" (DUO ANDRAS) sent to Peter. This detail is present in Sinaiticus and other early witnesses.

12. **Verse 40:** The phrase "having placed the knees" (QEIS TA GONATA) is a Semitic idiom meaning "having knelt." I preserve the literal Greek construction.

### Orthographic Notes

Sinaiticus employs standard fourth-century uncial conventions: nomina sacra throughout (abbreviated forms for Lord, Jesus, Christ, God, Spirit, etc.), continuous scriptio continua without word division, and occasional itacistic spellings characteristic of the scribe's hand.

-- EMET
